ecosystem: Salish Sea (Puget Sound, Strait of Juan de Fuca, San Juan Islands)
family: Orcinus orca -- Southern Resident population (J, K, L pods)

acoustic_nodes.description: Clicks up to 150 kHz. Multiple pod members echolocating simultaneously during active foraging: overlapping click trains create broadband acoustic presence in 10-150 kHz. Detection range for single large Chinook: 100-200 meters. Active sonar not communication; directional functional. Coordinated multi-whale echolocation during approach = most acoustically complex SRKW foraging event.

SRKW repertoire of species-specific calls with pod-specific dialects (each pod has distinct fine structure, distinguishable from other pods). Call rates increase maximum during active foraging: prey location, pod member positions, approach coordination. Matriarch's calls as organizational axis. Typically 1-15 kHz. Propagate through fjord waveguide.

Active foraging increases breathing rate: blows at surface more frequent. Above-water acoustic signature of active hunt: high-rate respiration sounds of multiple whales surfacing rapidly. Audible at the surface; part of the full acoustic event experienced from above water.

theory_nodes.concept: SRKW matriarchal social structure: pods organized around elder females, matriline as primary social bond. Post-reproductive females survive decades after reproductive capacity ends -- carry accumulated knowledge of salmon location (rivers, timing, depth by water column conditions). Brent et al. 2015 Current Biology: pods with older post-reproductive matriarchs showed higher survival during low-salmon years. Knowledge center role, not reproductive role.
theory_nodes.description: Matriarch as knowledge center -- post-reproductive female, salmon location memory, survival correlation.

theory_nodes.concept: Chinook salmon cultural prey specialization: SRKW preference is cultural not genetic, transmitted through matriarchal structure across generations. SRKW will pass abundant pink/coho/chum without foraging to find Chinook. The specialization produces the maximum cooperative hunt acoustics because Chinook are large and individually powerful, requiring maximum coordination and echolocation. ESA endangered 2005 listing; prey availability primary threat.
theory_nodes.description: Chinook cultural prey specialization -- transmitted matriarchally, produces maximum hunt acoustics.

theory_nodes.concept: Cooperative hunt acoustic architecture: detection (echolocation single-individual, 100-200m range), coordination (discrete calls maximum rate, pod member positions), approach (multi-whale overlapping echolocation, coordinated approach vectors surround prey), capture (individual Chinook not herding, echolocation brief cessation at jaw contact), food sharing (post-capture inter-pod sharing documented males with mothers). Complete acoustic event from detection through post-capture.
theory_nodes.description: Hunt sequence acoustic architecture -- five phases, each with distinct acoustic signature.

theory_nodes.concept: E=10 convergence calibration: multiple acoustic systems (echolocation, discrete calls, breathing) operating simultaneously at maximum rate, organized by matriarch's knowledge and vocalization. No single component uniquely extreme; convergence of all in coordinated maximum-foraging at peak Chinook season = E=10. Female expertise at operational center as both ecology and calibration rationale.
theory_nodes.description: E=10 -- convergence of three acoustic systems, matriarch coordination, female expertise at center.

cross_references.relationship: Both orca acoustic entries in the final six. 354: orca breach as single largest acoustic event. 357: orca cooperative hunt as multi-system acoustic event. Breach = detonation; hunt = sustained architectural coordination.

Full orca greeting (348) = social context calls, reunion, high-energy. Female-led hunt (357) = foraging context calls, maximum operational coordination. Same species, different behavioral contexts, different acoustic characters.

E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Female authority at operational center. SRKW matriarch: accumulated knowledge organizes pod. Kathleen Hanna: precision and urgency organizes movement. Two maximum-energy structures built around female expertise.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Female expertise at operational center of two maximum-energy structures. Matriarch knowledge organizes the hunt; Kathleen Hanna's articulation organizes the movement. Both about what female expertise produces at maximum.
